issue.title: All: Simplified English language names in locale settings
issue.body:
### Summary
This PR implements a long-standing **TODO** in the locale service to filter and simplify English language names.

### Description

The internal **ISO 639** list often contains exhaustive or technical names that can clutter the UI (e.g., "Spanish; Castilian" or "Greek, Modern (1453-)"). This change ensures that only the primary common name is displayed by:

- Splitting names at the first semicolon or comma.
- Removing parenthetical notes.
- Trimming extraneous whitespace.

This makes the language selection list much more approachable and professional for users across all platforms.

### Testing

- Logic verified against several complex entries in the ISO list.
- Confirmed that primary names are preserved while technical/secondary info is correctly stripped.

Two new public functions expose supported locales and language mappings. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Locale data sanitisation**
`packages/lib/locale.ts`|English name sanitisation logic added to ISO639 line processing, splitting on semicolons and commas, removing parenthetical content, and trimming whitespace before assignment to nameEnglish.| |**Public API expansion and tests**
`packages/lib/locale.test.ts`|New test cases for two newly exported functions: `supportedLocales()` and `supportedLocalesToLanguages()`, verifying return types and validating sanitised output contains no semicolons or year markers.|

performed_via_github_app.name: coderabbitai
performed_via_github_app.description:
# Transforming Code Reviews with AI

## Features

**Automated Reviews**: Continuous reviews of the pull requests including incremental commits. 

**Summarization**: Generates high-level summary and a technical walkthrough of the PR changes. 

**Line-by-line review**: Provides line-by-line suggestions committable with one click.

**Codebase verification**:  Verifies the impact on the overall codebase and identifies missing changes.

**Insights into your code**:  Ask any questions on your codebase within the pull request 

**Chat about your code** : Chat with the bot around your code. The more you chat, the smarter it gets.

**Issue Validation**:  Validates the PR against the linked issues and identifies other related issues

comments.body:
This PR needs serious testing. A before and after matrix would be useful.

I am most worried about the "same" languages that are spoken in different countries. e.g.
Portuguese in Portugal vs Brasil.
Spanish in Spain vs Latin America.
What about English? We have a separate entry for `English (US)`.

Also, I can't remember seeing any weird ISO numbers in the language dropdown list, unless something has changed and I didn't notice.
****CLA Assistant Lite bot**** All contributors have signed the CLA  ✍️ ✅
